reviews.review:
Fantastic RPG!

10/10 would use bread as a weapon again.
Quick little game if you're bored, very simple, decently fun. solid 8/10 if you're looking for a short game to play

Its a funny little RPG. Pretty easy though so nothing for a challenge. 
If you want to complete it 100% its about 10h.

Absolutly Worth the money even if it isnt on Sale

Fun game that takes heavy inspiration from Skyrim in the aspects of combat and quests whereas the humour tends to stem more from West of Loathing. You can usually take a guess as to where they've gotten the inspiration for different aspects of the game and I found myself enjoying it more because of that. 

It's a funny cute little game where you must level up and defeat different dungeon levels as you make the choice to fight for the king or against him in order to escape the island you've been trapped on. I loved it.

soup.
fun

I played the demo to completion and was really enjoying it. When it ended, it told me my progress would be saved for when I bought the full game so I could continue. 

When I bought and loaded into the full game, my save wasn't there. I didn't mind having to start again, but within the first five minutes I realized that my game was bugged. Items I was picking up were not disappearing from the screen, and upon getting my first level up, the skills screen was completely blank. 

I had to refund the game because this makes it unplayable. If these features are fixed I'd love to go back and play it again, but I can't recommend the full version in this state.
Great game. Very much like Supraland and Skyrim had a baby.

10 Hour Campaign if you're going for 100%
Bit of grinding at the end but other than that it's good.
Don't expect a 10/10 AAA experience, but expect an experience.

The game is good. But when I open the skill point menu there is nothing there. I've only started playing the game so I am not Max level. I thought it was because I had to reach a certain point but I've watched a person play it and they open it at level one and have lots of options. so I am trying to figure out how to fix it. I uninstalled it, restarted my Steam deck and loaded a new save file but still nothing there.
Great game. 10 hours of game play with just enough exploration and a sarcastic funny story that made me want to play and beat it in 2 sittings.
Fun, would recommend.
Amazing adventure

this game is very fun i would give it 4/5 stars. im at what i think is the final battle i left the dungeon to go get upgrads for equipment and found that i for some reason couldnt save? then found that the allies i was going to have for the fight were not spawning. i cant find guides online but this honestly has been a good game i have been playing for 13 hours. and at the price it was at it is worth the buy.

ps. if anyone finds a guide or can help me with figuring why im dealing with this problem i would probably make it a 5/5. its a short game but its fun and amuzing with the many refernces
Demo works perfectly fine (put 72 minutes into the demo). However, the full release crashed fifteen minutes into my session.
Pretty fun, but also very generic so far

Absolutely great game, simple, yet fun. 
The story is simple, but worth discovering. 
The gameplay feels very free, no hand holding, just pure fun and thinking outside of the box. 

I haven't fully finished it, as the Dragon is still killing me. 
But all in all, I can hands down recommend the game. 
Once you start, time will just fly.

The game soft lock you near end game... Only choice is to start over from the begining...
Edit : Also proceed to reset your settings from time to time...
Very fun game with many nods to other RPG's. Was a lot of fun.  Thank you for making such a great game.
Its the best.
Brilliant.  Cannot recommend it enough.
Best 8 hours shift ever
Unfortunately this game does not run on Linux. I got it to load a new game, but after about 2 minutes it would crash. Tried a few different times with the same results.
Fun

Fun little dungeon crawler with its own charm. Back to basics with lots of little side quest and humorous Npcs. No over bloat, no crazy inventory systems or management just loot, pick up, turn in. Had some lighthearted fun playing this game. Definitely enjoyed the game and the charm.

I do not recommend this game for anything more than $5 and even then, you might regret that purchase. It crashes constantly when you use your powers and attack at the same time, one of your powers being a freeze which encourages attacking while using it. Every time you alt tab out and alt tab black, the screen will stay black but don't worry you can still play the game, but only if you're blind and memorized where you are. The game will crash when using the elevator to one of the main systems of the game, the dungeon, and in that dungeon, you CANNOT save so you can end up losing probably 15 minutes of game time per crash. Also, in case you were getting way too comfortable at the end of the game,  [spoiler] the last 3 dungeons add enemies that won't take damage from 1 of your 2 (of 5 total) useful powers, fire breath, and cannot be frozen, the only other useful power, plus the last floor with the dragon, the enemies become twice as powerful just because as i can imagine, the developer just wants to pad the play time.  [/spoiler] In terms of performance, the game runs fine but that is only because every asset pops in when you are 20 feet away from it, with no way to change this. While this game is funny and not too bad in the first 30 minutes, it's safe to say the rest of the time is a waste.
It was a good fun time.  Well worth the price.  Silly and somewhat simple, but fun
Its all for the loot.

Ragdoll physics combat, very simple version of not-Skyrim that does it all right.
Darker than it seems, a bit like Bonelab... 

monsters are just a faction of NPC and NPC know it. But they still harass them, given how monsters tries to flee, to build wall to lock themelves out of reach in the dungeon. Also monsters semm to have children. While official NPC don't. It's apaprently made to mess up with our head and it works to an extent.

So this it's absolutely not wholesome, or even sane if you think about the island seriously.
Noice!
Fun game 4/5
Its just awesome in every aspect.
Classic Sit Back and Chill game.
Absolutely hilarious.
Any fan of Elder Scroll games will enjoy this goofy and fun adventure.
Needs a lot of polishing, but quite fun
Fun
The skills is not showing up it black  maybe the last update broke it

Demo was great; felt like Supraland and humour is good (overall really, not a fault).

Release product is barely fit for purpose.

Performance is meh, although LoD/pop in is absolutely horrid as you leave the first village.
A certain end-game fight is horrible and most end-game combat is so damn tedious; which I get may be part of the experience but it stands out after what was a solid intro demo. 

Full price? No way this is worth. Wait for a sale and some QoL/graphics updates.
Bread

If you are a fan of RPGs and are aware of how crappy the gaming industry has gotten, this game is a breath of fresh air. It goes out of the way to show how aware of itself the game is. This is NOT a serious narrative driven game, but more of a funny and engaging spoof on what RPGs are these days. The gameplay itself is pretty basic but the game does not waste your time, so you don't feel like you are putting effort into something that will not pay off. The pay off is to see where the writers are taking the story and what wacky stuff is going to happen next. 

I promise if you are tired of replaying the GOATs over and over again, this is worth the purchase.

Take Skyrim, combine it with Supraland, make it infinitely dumber (in a good way) and that's this game. The graphics may not be the best, but they're charming. The game may have a few bugs, but that just makes it better. 9.5/10 You should play this game.
Fun
A fun 8-12 hour journey. Similar in play combat wise to the first Supraland. A few bugs, but nothing overly gamebreaking
Привет, когда подбираешь предметы они не исчезают, и самое главное невидно навыков просто пусто. Играю на стим дек.
It was a good time, the king was pretty cool.
It dumb. It fun.
I wish there was a mixed review option. Because although this game has little depth and has potential to be so much more than it's current state. I had fun playing it for what it is. 6/10
Well, I was sold at "NO FISHING MINI-GAMES." The rest was just extra. Jokes aside, the game was lovely — though I did wait an hour for a bread sword to cook. so 9.876 out of 10
Mid. It was fun at first but rapidly loses its charm, conveniently post-refund period. 5/10 game.

I like how you can legit rob anyone. I mugged a dude's grandma and pickpocketed her while she was unconscious. You can even mug the king. He's only like lvl 3 but his guards will mess you up. You can also shoot mushrooms like megaman/Samus. Its a pretty funny game. Especially with the jiggle physics.
A mechanically shallow game with a simplistic artstyle and story, that doesn't take advantage of its parodies. Game length stretched out for the sake of grinding.

I had a really great and fun time with this game! 

First learned of this game through the steamer: Joov, and was curious. Love it when Demos are available, cause once I tried it, I figured it was actually fun enough to buy. Played it till the end, and noticed there are more endings I could try. May not get around that anytime soon, but I had a great time and anytime I have a Skyrim fix that I want to do ( but not quit after 2hrs) , I'll definitely comeback to retry this again!

Solid game, though very repetitive. I got 100% in just over 9 hours, which may have dragged on a bit long for the gameplay variety, but not by an unreasonable amount. The developers very clearly put a lot of love into the game, and it benefits from it greatly. I am very much in the target audience for a game like this so my only real complaint i have is that the price might be a little high unless you really think you'll vibe with it

I 100%ed this game and had a blast. Its silly, simple fun. I was actually surprised by just how enthralled i was playing it and how stubborn i was to do everything. 
I dont think ive ever 100%ed a game in my life lol

The game design was solid too. I didnt run into any major bugs thankfully, and when i did because i was messing around with the windmill, i just restarted the game, loaded my save, and i was fine. The developer has a good sense of game design and level design. One room full of enemies that you go through to progress, had a door to the left with a passcode, and a door to the right. Many players likely might have gone through the left door and continued on through. But by putting a passcode on it, it forces players to go through the right door, which ended up being a saferoom with a respawn point and the code. This way, if the player died, they could respawn away from the enemies in the next room. That was a smart move on the Dev's part. The quests are fun too, and the different powers you unlock can make traversing the island fun until you unlock all the portals. I found myself going everywhere i could just to see if i can. The top of the Castle, across the broken bridge before it was time, to a large island to the southwest that didnt have anything on it except the satisfaction of figuring out how to get there...

Its a good game.

I love the design and idea behind the game but hard to recommend when its so broken on the steam deck. I purchased the game after playing the demo, and some how from the demo to the full version things broke badly. 

Loot was no longer disappearing when collected regardless of it showing I had it (buggy but not game breaking). The skills menu is completely blank so no way of spending my skills, making combat extremely difficult (game breaking). Talking to the guard in the tavern allowed infinite loot to spawn. The drunken adventure in the dungeon entrance keeps taking the ticket without the quest moving forward. The sidekick that follows me is walking in thin air above me, not allowing him to engage in combat (in the dungeon he appears above the ceiling). My main weapon doesn't reach the enemy anymore (broke after getting the hammer). No achievements are unlocked regardless of completing them.

Verified that moving my save to my pc fixes most issues (haven't checked them all) but did not purchase this game for this reason, wanted something mobile to play at slow times at work and seeing that this was suppose to be verified on Steam Deck.

The RPG is a parody of other RPG games. It's fun at first, for 2 first hours. But then the game remains extremely shallow and repetitive quests: go to dungeon, kill some dude, fetch some item.

Because the main game idea is a parody, there's very barebones mechanics, almost no story, and no reason for any of the quests, adding to the shallow feeling. Playing the entire second half of the game was quite a chore.

From technical side game also lacks. Graphics are below basic, but game still runs at 40 fps in more open areas. While CPU/GPU utilization stays at 50%. Why can't the game use 100% GPU to reach proper FPS? There were also some gameplay bugs.

Overall, game idea is cool, but execution very subpar.

There are good things and fun to be had here, but I would not buy it until there are bugfixes.

There are states a player can get into where jumping breaks. This basically breaks some portions of the game and the only remedy is to exit the game completely and restart.  This is bad because the player can't save in dungeons and the entire endgame is considered a dungeon meaning huge progress may be lost.

There are also states where quests are bugged. I have one quest where the NPC refuses to talk about it because they're waiting on another quest. This means I cannot progress that quest at all nor the achievement. Quests in general have many UX bugs that really should not exist in a finished product; these are all things other developers faces and solved decades ago, so it's odd that they should appear here. It's not just one quest or situation either; there are a number of quests that are broken like this (can't you just if/else or switch to see if the player has some open quests?)

To complete said blocking quest as well, a bunch of grinding is required. I need 25 of each type of bread but the UI doesn't show me any separation of what I have at all? How am I to know what the composition of the 30-something I have on hand are? The gold needed also requires that grinding.

I see the love and the humor behind it, but I found as much frustration as fun. I wanted to do another playthrough making other choices, but instead I'm probably not going to finish the final part of the game at all.

Story is fun and the dungeon levels were well thought out.   Semi-open world is interesting and lots of easter eggs.

Abilities/perks are pretty basic.  A good casual play that's worth the price.

Had alot of fun with this game ... until the end .... The game broke and I could no longer jump. I tried all sorts of stuff and it did not matter. The last two levels of progress lost and I am not uninstalling to reinstall ( hoping that solves the problem ) ( restarts did nothing ) hard locked by a bug.

Great game if that was  not an issue anymore. Refund requested.

Having 100%ed this game, I believe it was worth it. This is a silly little parody of Skyrim. What you see is what you get.

My biggest criticism would be the unneeded hour long wait for the battle bread. By the time it is available, you've most likely done everything else. It already was so costly and requires the later step anyways, so was making me go AFK needed?
It's alright; humor, memes and silly mechanics. Rich with bugs and soft-lockability so be prepared, it's open world but if you progress non-linearly you will have tasks you cannot complete.

This game haha! While it might not be perfect in any way it's damn good fun. It makes fun of itself just as much as of other games and it's pure heart and love. I *cough* "borrowed" the game before buying it and it only took minutes to realize that the game was well worth it's money!

This game was good fun!  This game will not blow your mind.  It is not going to change what you expect going forward for RPG's. 

What it does though is it takes you on a fun adventure through a VERY tongue, and cheek adventure.  This game knows exactly what it is, and exactly what you expect it to be.  So it is going to give it to you, and then it is going to ... well spoilers.

A short adventure packed full of predictable, and very much NOT predictable things to do.  You will meet other heroes, villians, and a big bad in the end?  A fun game that gives you what you want, and at the same time giving you far more then expected.  Well worth your time, and money.  I can not wait to see what else they come up with!
wonderful game. i really enjoyed the calm and nice atomsphere. its funny and fun.
Fun humor, kinda has west of loathing vibes but for a more skyrim/oblivion experience.  Plenty of laughs but doesn't over stay it's welcome.
Skyrim footsteps on a budget, but even better than the OG.
Mid game. You may think its just fun jokes on your avg rpg. And it dose that to some extent. But for atleast half of the time its grind simulator with bad combat.

was having fun playing this, but then it kept crashing after reaching a certain point.  I just bought the final sword and just received the final quest to make the special sword, but when I try to use the elevator with a dungeon ticket the game froze every time.  not sure if the developer is going to fix this or if I should try my luck by starting a new game.

Not really a thumbs up or a thumbs down, just kinda "meh".
Don't think ill be coming back to this title.

Picture this: Goat simulator that tries to take itself seriously, that's this game.
Jokes & actual melee combat with levels, stats, stamina bars & critical hits.

In this game there is 2 types of currency, loot & money.
Everything you pick up has a loot value, you can only sell loot for money, 15-20 per 1$. 
Money can be spent on ammo or upgrades.

Combat is pretty easy to get good at.
You've got your basic melee, charged melee, block, ranged attack in a form of a mushroom gun & shouts like in skyrim.
Most of said shouts suck, personally only used freeze, fire & a little bit of slowing down time during the final boss.
Melee is kinda clunky especially if you have a party member to fight alongside with, more often than not ill be the one accidentally K.O:ing them rather than hitting the enemy.
Mushroom gun is kinda there, useful if you want to cheese a fight or fight at a range, but it doesn't deal insane amounts of damage.
The scaling is a bit all over the place, better get used to fighting enemies close to your level only to end up fighting a boss with a skull next to their name to indicate the level difference rly isnt in your favor.

The game doesn't have a proper inventory system so everything is either there on the bottom of your screen or gets added to an endless bag.
This is honestly a very stupid design choice as some of these items are only used like once or twice & they'll forever stay at the bottom of your screen.
Also this type of inventory system clumps all of the healing items into 1 icon & there's 3 different types of healing items that each heal for a different amount. 
And the best part, 1 of the quests requires you to gather 25 of each of the healing items, how do you know how many items of each you have? 
Guess or buy 25 new ones.

The quests are okish for the most part. "Go here & do this thing" "kill X number of B"
Get a more confusing quest & want to read up on it a bit? Tough luck, there's no quest texts & this game is a pretty niche title, so good luck finding any proper guides online.
Also good luck figuring out which npc's have a quest for you as they don't really stand out from the crowd with a ! or even try to flag the player down to give their quest.
So to not miss any quests you're better off breaking into everyones homes & talking to everyone multiple times.
I thoroughly enjoyed this game!!! If you're looking for an RPG that is fun to play, but doesn't take itself too seriously, this is the game for you! Sadly the Discord link is not valid though

very nice game, remind me alot of Supraland in the vipes and the way the game is played, i highly recommend it, but even as fun as it is, icant recommend it at full price if u on a budged but if you got cash to spare its worth it on full price
This game is much fun anyone who loves skyrim will get all the cool references

Full review found here: https://youtu.be/w8d6L6NzBys

The RPG thrives on humor and simplicity. It begins with an absurd cart accident thanks to the Glitchy Dragon and never looks back, featuring an island centered around a pay-to-play dungeon where everything comes at a cost. From your first steps into town, you’re greeted with playful nods to classic RPG tropes. Guards sport permanent arrows in their knees, shopkeepers sell torches for poorly lit dungeon floors, and every trip into the depths requires a literal ticket.

Combat is refreshingly straightforward. You hit enemies with whatever weapon you have, step out of range, and repeat. Occasional Dragon Screams give you brief moments of power, and the Mushroom Launcher is available if you want to keep your distance, though it feels less impactful without heavy upgrades. Leveling progress is steady, with rewards like gold stars that you can invest in passive skills, while loot piles up everywhere, turning every pot, mushroom, or trinket into eventual coin.

Outside the dungeon, the island feels lively despite its small size. Helping stranded townsfolk and completing simple quests break up the grind, while the quirky writing and visual jokes keep the tone light. The art style enhances the humor, with clear character expressions and environments that feel polished even when poking fun at genre clichés.

The story doesn't rely on emotional stakes but instead offers a playful satire of how adventure economies might function if someone monetized every step. It's entertaining, witty, and clever enough to keep you smiling through most encounters. Replay value is modest; once you've seen the jokes and completed the quests, you might only return to grind for gold to fully upgrade your Mushroom Launcher. Yet for a focused, satirical take on dungeon crawling, The RPG hits exactly where it aims: fun, fast, and self-aware.

Sadly after 3 hours of trying to get the game to not crash, the developer will not allow a refund as I've spent over 2 hours on the game. As such, I will be rating the game as a negative.

I'd probably avoid this until it leaves early access.. Oh.. Damn it's not an early access title. Shit. That's really not good guys.

Please be aware of possible spoilers in my review. 
I mean I'd probably recommend it, but I'd recommend it more with couple of minor changes, such as the maker improving the loot to gold conversion to make the game play a bit faster paced and rewarding, especially towards the endgame. I got super bored of going around and around the Dungeon floors, the Island and hitting the practice dummy hundreds of times to get loot, only for it to convert into very little gold for upgrades...as such I went into late game under powered, died a bunch and have just totally stalled because I don't want to go around again to get loot to convert into very little gold for basic supplies, let alone armour upgrades etc.
Also checkpoints that aren't just bonfires would be great for significant fights too. For example if the player dies after destroying the machine, it would be great if the fight picks up from after the machine is destroyed. Having to destroy it again, and fight all the machines again, before the Boss three times became sooooooooo tedious I just gave up on it and as of right now have no plans to revisit this game.
I think it's a pretty good game with decent graphics, it made me laugh a lot with the silly humour and little Easter eggs honouring other games, I just think the pacing would be improved with those little tweaks to make the game a bit less taxing to play with the constant backtracking.
Fun little RPG with satire. Interested to see a follow up with more of a budget and developers.
Cool
It's like Skyrim for kids.  But, not for kids (well, maybe for my kids ^^).  Very fun wander-n-slay with a lot to do!

It's not bad, but most of the fun and interesting things are discovered in the 1.25 hour demo. Beyond that it feels like speedrunning a very mid RPG. I appreciate that the pacing is fast, dungeons and quests are quick, but there is just not much to them. It gets bland quickly. At least there is the occasional humorous moment or cool secret area to find.

If you are considering buying, wait for a deep discount.
I liked the game. Simple fun some humor and likable elements. However, it has crashed 5 times on loading screens which is especially tedious in dungeons where progress is not saved.
Got very bland.

The game is neat. Not too long for it to become stale, but at the endpoint reaches some uncanny resemblance of anything that it tries to parody in a bad way.

And when you start, DO NOT PICK the hardest difficulty. The most miserable second to last boss fights from the end of the game that I've experienced in a long while.

its a fun game, but its very quick with both player progression and story progression, so replay-ability isn't very high. I still had a great time playing and a good laugh pretty much the entire time. I Highly recommend playing for its price.
needs voice acting

I'm going with yes, but on sale.

The RPG is a casual adventure with RPG elements in which you hope to be overwhelmed with jokes and puns, but ends feeling a bit slow and like it's dragging its feet around the end. The end game enemies are nigh impossible without upgrades and become trivial with. The game does provide you enough resources to acquire the best weapon and armor without the need to extra grind, which I am very thankful for.

Everything you loot is literally loot which you sell for gold (20 loot = 1 gold, depending on the item you get different amounts of loot when picking them up) with which you buy everything. Healing items (bread), weapon and armor upgrades and mushroom machine gun upgrades (yes, it's a thing). Combat is very straight forward. Enemies walk toward you and play out an animation that allows you to easily sidestep or just circle or backstep most enemies. Bosses usually one shot you if you don't have upgrades. Criticals make them fall in 3 or so hits. I think it definitely needs improvement in how it feels.

The final battles are a bit of a challenge considering most of the combat situations you encounter beforehand. There's a dungeon with different floors that you explore to complete missions or clear out of enemies. You can also help the townsfolk, farmers, the Cool King himself and other characters in this world. There's a few locations to explore, and you can catapult around at first, but then unlock portals to fast travel. For what it's worth, I did enjoy looking for the townsfolk and their quests, and the fact that the first time you're running around the island there's a lot of easter eggs and references strewn about.

I didn't expect to have the option to choose how most quests ended, you can either comply with your instructions or opt for an antagonistic alternative and the game would react accordingly. The Dragon even comments on it later on. This Dragon serves as the antagonist and will sometimes comment on your progress through the dungeon's floors.

It was a good 9 hours, but if I knew what I was doing they'd probably have been about 7 which is the score I'll give this game.
Trol and funny. Good humor. i enjoy playing it
Total hidden gem worth the price

wow.. just wow. Played 8 hours to the final boss. Final boss was WAYY too tanky. basically running in circles for an hour. 

Finally beat it and.... game breaking bug where loading screen doesn't go away. I had to sit back and physically clap to see how this semi-decent game had its entire experience ruined for me due to this. The pinnacle of the game and there's a game breaking bug. 

So, sadly, after that experience, I have to not recommend this game because I was unable to technically 'beat' it due to this bug. Wish I could refund it due to that it dissapointed me that much.

interesting game until its not anymore. I liked it, had fun for a bit, faced some bugs that messed things up, used some bugs to my advantage which was fun. Wouldn't recommend the game since you dont really get much out of it, feels to messy and stuff arent really calculated to be balanced.

this game is 100% worth buying this is a really fun RPG where you can just do whatever you want and have a little NPC buddy helping you until lvl 10 makes me feel like im playing coop or something its just alot of fun

Well..you can have some fun with it 
But overall game is raw and feels like early access
very rpg
Fun, jank, and a respectable amount of playtime to offer. A great game if you love to smash every pot and box that you run across or have the need to grab everything that isn't nailed to the ground.
10/10 game. It was tons of fun and had some great callbacks to Skyrim. Worth the price.

Decent game, kind of weird scaling, you do alot of damage on some occasions but fall off very quick against certain levels. Also wish it auto saved more as i lost nearly an hour of progress due to game crash.
It'll make you laugh and it's pretty fun to play.
was great having a blast then i walk threw a door in the village and no its just brown and like a sky cant move or do much and its stuck game breaking bug.almost 6hr gone

I'm honest, I expected a little more from this game from what I've seen before. It starts nice, it has some funny elements and some nice references to other games and feels a little like Supraland. But the funny references and puzzles stop quite fast and the game turns into a grind fest. Slay monsters and collect loot, sell it, get better gear, level up to get stronger (the latter is implemented completely stupid, you only get 3 skill points each level, but new skills will later cost more than three points, but you can't save your points from the level up before, you just lose them...) It gets really repetitive fast. The end boss fight is one of the dumbest and most annoying I've ever encountered and the end of the game is incredibly unsatisfying, Unfortunately the game does exactly what it makes fun of in other games...
I don't really want to downvote the game, it's a small game from one developer and it's well made. It has some bug here and there and could use some more polish, but it's fine, especially for the price. But to sum it up: It looks funnier than it is...
As long as you expect a comedic short experience, preferably under the influence, this game is great. Took me 8 hours to 100% and I'd be down to play more genre parody games from this creator.
Great simple fun, minecraft style combat and more than a few laughs. Would fly across a bridge in style again! (I may have gone back and forth a few times while chuckling :) )

So I will start out with a short summary for those wanting a brief breakdown is this game worth buying? Yes it is for the story elements HOWEVER completing the game from the monsters side will give you quite the headache. The game itself can be completed in 5 hrs if done at a decent pace, 7hrs if you are taking your time.

I 100% the game in 11 with 1 hr of it MANDATORY sitting. The difficulty spiked out of control near the end of the game if you wanted to side with the monsters and go to ??? to help them. We are talking almost double your level. Not to mention the money needed to comfortably get gear was... so grindy. The game was so enjoyable up to that point which is to say 90% of the game! So for those wanted a great laugh and to have many moments of pure knee slaps its amazing. 

There are several guides out there atm with horrible tips please don't follow them. Just enjoy the game and hit the hardest floors you can as often as it allows there is a in game timer for respawns. Don't feel you will mess up by playing both sides. I was able to get ALL achievements with one playthrough however the breadsword one i didnt find out about till later later and had to wait a in real life hour so PLEASE safe yourself and hit that early it IS a bit bugged so keep buying 5 at a time and keep talking to him it will eventually pop (even though I had to buy like 200 bread...) 

Final notes: The game has NOTHING left for you to do after completing all achievements so take your time and enjoy exploring and finding all the secret loctations. Money in the game is HARD so get every piece of loot you can grab and HORDE it till you can sell at the cave. All in all a solid 8/10
Love this game.  Very cute and the adventure is so fun.

Its fine. It was cheap at $11 or whatever it was. The game did crash once but otherwise I didnt experience any bugs or performance issues.

I wish there was magic other than the 'shouts'. Its not particularly complicated, but thats also why it was only $11. Will I get 11 hours out of it, probably not, but youll spend the same going to McDonalds for dinner ....

There is a lot of tounge-in-cheek humor... obvious Skyrim references and some other game references. 

I found the combat to be really easy to cheese... sprint slash sprint slash... the sprinting increases your crit chance and if you rank up your crit chance and crit damage... pretty simple.

100%: 6-10 Hours
This is an extremely fun and well thought out 3D RPG Adventure which'll keep you around for a while, as you tie up loose ends to quests and enjoy the witty dialogue, as well as the quest and level designs. 
The features are simple yet intuitive, and holds true toward the shroud of satire The RPG loves to live in, and I'm all here for it, I think it's a hilarious breath of fresh air.
I had a ton of fun messing around with every part of the game, embracing it's well polished jankiness (which is a funny concoction of words but for a game this satirical and driven on it's nostalgia to some degree, it feels right) into the product it is today.
Congrats to everyone that worked on this game, you created a great experience that can be played in a ton of different ways. Just a great time all around!

this game, tries to make fun of RPG's mechnics and be silly by doing all the stuff it make fun of, my game crush multiple times and i had to reload the game few more because stuff stopped working, there is grind stats don't make much sense, but if you don't do well it will be hard. i beat the game, and it was fine, but if someone asked me if they should play it i would say no
Fun but very short. If you like parodies, this one is for you - However, I finished the game in 8hrs (all quests including side quests)... so it's kind of a one and done type of game.

With potentially satisfying combat, and fairly high quality minimalist open-world RPG gameplay.  The game is fairly fun, paying tribute to other games (Elderscrolls, etc) while having its own quirky story. (I need to replay and see what the monsters have to say...)  Gameplay is the highlight here...

I probably wouldn't recommend it compared to the games it parodies...  But it's not a bad game at all, just a lite RPG romp, with some fun elements.  Your actions seem to have minimal consequences most of the time.    Recommended if you are looking for a lite comical Skyrim inspired parody-ish RPG.

The world is fairly small, and enemies do not scale with you.  The overworld areas were basically a cakewalk, while the dungeon floors held the thrills.  I played as a damage glass cannon build, so most of the game I was getting one-shotted by tougher opponents, and that kept me on my toes...  I was basically all melee, relying on charging the enemy to launch a critical, then carefully dodge out of harms way, or eventually learn to perfect block...  Oh and the Dragon was by far my favorite character and battle in this game.  Very chill and random. xD

Anyway, I played SANABI before this, so for me the story was very lacking...  But, it was still a good easy going combat focused RPG.  I'd say a $9.99 value imo?

Oh, I did run into occasional weird glitches while playing though, like dead enemies following me around... but it kinda fits the glitchy setting lol.  Also crashed once during loading, though it autosaves at fair intervals when outside the dungeon.
One of the cutest and funnest games I've played in awhile. It was a great filler for a week of enjoyment. Nice cleanser from the AAA titles i play.

I really enjoyed this game. It was made with so much love and often makes you smile. I was pretty sad when it was over. That's why it pains me to mention this criticism: the grinding towards the end is exhausting. When you want the most powerful gear, but your only option is to repeat the dungeons you've already done countless times, or to spend hours beating up the training dummy. It was pretty tiring. I intended to collect all the achievements, but I made the mistake of accidentally skipping the last quest because there was no warning that there was no going back once you used the key. And to play the whole game over again for that, even though I enjoyed it so much? No, the grind was too hard. How could it have been made better? I don't know, maybe with some kind of endless tower where there's more variety or something. But enough of the complaining, this game is worth buying. It was a lot of fun and i hope there will be more in the future!
Short, cute, funny little game. It's not perfect (especially if you like using a controller), but it fulfills it's promise at a good price-per-hour.
yes
help the game somehow switched to portugese and i cant change it back bc i dont know what settings is in portugese LMAO

Even though it's basically just a parody of many other RPGs of its ilk, it's also a ton of fun. So many things to do around the lands, so many places with interesting characters, secrets, and a clear path to getting stronger. 

It's a lot of fun.
So addicting i beat it in one night and ruined my sleep schedule for the foreseeable future
amazing game great art style would recommend
Goodgame, GreatGAme

A simple and fun little rpg, really the worst I can say about it is the lack of quests (or ways to find them) but honestly even then grinding is pretty easy. Heck the fact that when I did bug reports and were answered very quickly is enough to give this a thumbs up

I highly recommend this game to anyone who likes RPGs. If you are a fan of Skyrim and / or other RPGs this game does poke fun at them a little bit. Simpler mechanics and addicting game play make you come back for more.
It's fun, but getting to the ending can be a drag, due to prices.

This is one of those "I wish Steam had a sideways thumb" moments. The game isn't BAD it just isn't any good either. I get that it's supposed to be a parody game, but there needs to be a game in a parody game. This is just...I don't know.

It's a giant nothing burger. Honestly that's worse that being objectively bad to me, so it means instead of a recommended "meh" it's a not recommended "meh".

This is exactly the game you are looking for when you are completely fed up with long questlines, grind and altogether bad game design.

Game design is a tricky thing, dev of this game had realised that there is a much needed niche for games that actually play well, are simple and are just fun. Which a lot of huge studios absolutely don't grasp.

In that philosophy he wanted to parody our beloved ultra grinders and hard games to show us how ridiculous they actually are. A fantastic observation. He managed to create a simple action RPG thats not a chore but a simple manifestation of the genre like in the imagination of people in the D&D community in the 80s when everybody playing the game is slightly drunk.

I love it, it was a blast.
Supraland meets Skyrim, great fun and much more content than I was expecting
It's a quick play at under seven hours, but the laughs are worth it and the mechanics keep it fun and interesting. It will keep you laughing and bring back memories of the games it "honors"

This was a fun game. the writing was actually funny but also just good enough to pay attention too and the game-play was not too tedious to be frustrating. the difficulties are good enough to change your game playing style while not over frustrating you. also, I love the game who name we shall not mention that this might be a parody of. worth the price.
Very fun, was captivated, I'm at the last mission now, so 6.5 hrs if you include my time from the demo, but I'm having a blast!
This game is very simple and just plain fun! I'm having a blast and thats how a game should be.

Immediately dragged me in with some jokes and game references. The overall story and 100% achievements isnt that long but that is fine. Some of these references made me chuckle, the game wasnt a boring grind and not too chaotic either, I would say just about the right balance. Worth my money
Took the first 5 seconds to get me excited.

I have to say as a person that developes games myself, this is solid work.
There is a lot you can add on to in time if you wish to make a dlc or even multiplayer aspects of it.
Overall, this is a blast to play. 10/10
The RPG is a fun casual game that is pretty fun. I just wish it had a more searchable name :)

Totally unexpected for me, but this is very cool, simple, not annoying at all, inexpensive indie RPG for 10-11 hours (this is taking into account the grind at the end, because it's very difficult to bring down the final boss without top loot, and hunting all achivements during the game).
It's good but why? Don't know.... worth playing but why?? I do not know... Just try it yourself.

Well it runs into the key problem that loads of comedy games run into: it's just not funny. Bonus it also tries to have meta-commentary on games and humor, like turning all pick-up-ables into "loot" which you can pawn for money, but that just makes the game shallow. There's so little interesting about the game, world, and now the loot... you end up skipping most of what the world has to offer and the items on the ground. You're left with doing the same left click attack over and over as you slowly upgrade its attack over time and sometimes buy an expensive part for your mushroom gun so it can shoot a mushroom into space instead of at the monster.

I never give bad reviews and the game was kind of fun, but then in the late game, I made a progress of 7 levels and collected tons and tons of loot (11k+), only to get a bug that permanently twisted my controls and made the game unplayable, about 30 minutes into a ridiculously long boss fight. Even dying did not reset the controls, so I had to leave the game and lose hours of my time, all levels I worked for, and all 11k of loot. Worst experience ever!
Great game. Simple RPG that just brought on the nostalgia. Laughed a ton. Totally worth every penny.
Do you like Skyrim? Do you like Supraland? Do you like mushrooms? If you answered yes to ANY of these questions you need to play The RPG. Honestly just buy it.  You'll like it.
it good
Simple, fun, addictive

A lot of fun but could definitely be more, I was just getting into the mechanics when I realized I was over halfway through the floors of the dungeon and the end game was just a bit of grinding for an easy finish. Would love to see some expansions that add more to the story or world. Maybe get that fishing mini-game I've heard so much about.
Fun game.

Overall a very fun and creative game that like others have mentioned scratches the supraland itch a bit. In terms of content for me personally, it took about 9 hours to come close to completing (Achievement hunting a little here now) There is definitely a limit to the content, but for the price it feels pretty reasonable. Especially in a market saturated with triple A titles that feel like I could take it or leave it, this was definitely a refreshing piece to be had. I will say I had some smaller complaints with having to really grind later, but in a sense, it's also nice because you can really see the difference before and after that growth. Oh, and there's apparently multiple endings. Too da loo!
good game, funny references and actual unique ending with a little twist
15 dollar game for quality 20 hours of content. absolutly worth it.

The game is a typical atypical RPG. It is very well thought out, satisfying, and provides a great deal of enjoyment. What's more, the game does not have tedious tasks, it is interesting, and the storyline is quite good. A downside is the lack of use of the castle and the fact that there is nothing in the towers, which is a bit disappointing. It would also be nice to have automatic item pickup.
i don't like bethesda games but i love this

Honestly this is way better than I was expecting. I don't want to say a lot and spoil anything cos I went in blind and loved every second, but this one is going on the list of new releases I've had the most fun with so far this year.
After beating the game (and collecting 1000 mushrooms) I have finally 100% the game. Very fun game.

Lightheartedly poking fun at RPGs with some peculiar twists and turns. Absurdity and humor are needed breaks from serious real-world issues. Def. interested in dev’s future game-titles despite normally not being into minimalistic games. Collect a huge number of mushrooms!

Great little game that doesn't take it self too seriously. The only thing i would add would be some battle music when you fighting and a boss music when fighting bosses, the sound track is quite bland.
If you like the humor found in Lego games, and you've played too much Skyrim, this game is for you.
Nice fun single player Rpg. I wish it lasted longer

The RPG.
It's simply The RPG. You like RPG's? You'll love The RPG!

Game is a lot of fun and hjooks you with its sillyness and simplicity. I like that there are 2 different routes you can explore either following the cool king or the glitched dragon. I have faced quite a few bugs some of which made me restart the game and loose a lot of progress because saving isnt allowed in dungeons. if you can tolerate that i recommend the game
Funny goofy game. I refunded it though because it just didn't hold my attention long enough.

I made an immediate purchase for this game after playing the demo, which gave me vibes of Skyrim, Supraland, and a bit of West of Loathing.

It's a very simple game that is an absolute joy to play while laughing at all the satire, irony, and hidden references to many games.  It has some bugs, but the developer works on it constantly with daily patches fixing the most well reported issues.

Absolutely recommended

It’s fun, I just wish it ran well on the Deck. In Dungeons it’s fine, but it doesn’t run very well in any exterior and stutters to the point that it makes you feel a bit sick. I recommend it but I do wish it had some tweaking for the deck, or at the very least had settings you could change yourself.
This is the Skyrim re-re-re-re-re-re-re-re-re-re-re-release we've been waiting for.

I have been enjoying this game a lot. However I will be taking a break from this game until the bugs are fixed. I just lost a LOT of progress due to the game being unable to save. Not because of a dungeon or something, but solely because the game stopped allowing me to save or load a save. Instead the options are unavailable without any explanation. Another side quest line is entirely broken for me to the point I can't progress it, but I could look past that.
It was a funny adventure, i had bought 2 copies for our family to play. We all beat it and got every achievement to unlock. Cant wait for more adventures to come.
It's been a great adventure. Loved it, and will replay it for what I assume is a different ending.
Приятная игра

while I enjoy the game in its normal form, I feel the need to complain about the awful decision of not being able to save at bonfires, meaning, if your game crashes mid dungeon or you have to get off the game, a lot of your progress can be lost. I ran into this problem at the last two dungeons, meaning around 1 and a half hours of hard gameplay were lost.

Dionysus Acroreites I really need more games like this so please make more. I love The RPG simple combat yet the humour is very entertaining. I bought this game on a sale and figured i'll try it out, to my surprise there's so much to do when it comes to missions and exploration.

I highly recommend The RPG to anyone with a sense of humour and wants something that is whacky and fun. 😊👍
the king is so cool you guys

Overall fun game, you could easily beat the game pretty quickly, maybe about 5 hours. 
But there's plenty of content to this game and just hidden things to push you to explore, especially if you're going for 100% achievements collected. 

Dev seems pretty active with the community and is fixing any bugs extremely quick, huge plus. 

9/10
its so good i went to heaven and saw God while playing it

I love this silly little game. Jokes and references from Skyrim, cool mechanics and dungeons, chars are simple, but memorable. And the voice lines are like Meh meh meh meh, reminds me of Charlie Brown parents. Just good fgun. 
Oh and if you like RPGs without inventory Management...

it's just kinda shallow and not really funny. like maybe this would've been funny 10 years ago?

awesome

Just finished the game pretty fun, but I had a few issues with quests and when i entered the laboratory i was unable to exit back to the island, so I had to turn down the difficulty and steamroll the final boss to not be softlocked. I don't know if you can exit the dungeon any other way than teleporters and elevators. I think it should be a button in the menu to exit the dungeon.

A few comments:
* I wish it leaned more heavily into skyrim, the talent points are kind of bland. 
* I wish there was magic spells and more types of weapons
* Bombs felt like a gimmick and did not have any specific purpose except for clearing one barricade with them
* The grind was a bit tedious at the end to get strong enough to beat the dragon. Being able to be friends with the monsters was a nice touch. 
* It was not obvious that destroying the tree was a permanent action. It softlocked a few of my quests.
* I really enjoyed stealing and slaughtering everyone. Especially the Cool king. Over and over. :devil:  

I still think the game is a 8/10 it scratches that Supraland itch really well.

It definitely has its moments and is overall pretty fun.  Worth 10-15 bucks, for sure.

Lots of bugs, but the dev is working constantly to fix them.  Biggest problem is that later game enemies/bosses are way too spongy, but I expect that will get adjusted too in balance patches.

Quite enjoyable

It's bugs froze my PC, softlocked myself or did silly things like removing my jump. Thankfully the Dev is patching the game constantly, meaning I wake up the next day and see that a game breaking bug is fixed now. About a month from now, I imagine this game will be bug-less, or as bug-less as it can be.

It's gameplay is simple and minimalist, as it advertizes itself to be, and it hyper focuses the game into quests, enemy design, and tone. Figuring out the best way to take care of enemies and bosses was enjoyable, and while I could just constantly upgrade my mushroom shooter and spend all my money on ammo, making every fight a cakewalk, I also think that's a choice you make as a gamer so i'm fine with it.

The game is silly and that sustains itself for the most part. The quest design allowed for a lot of levity, though near the end, as the quests started to lessen, the game entered into the grind zone.

The ending section of the game got quite crazy, even with the hints leading up to it. I also like the little twist it plays by the final boss fight. Won't spoil, enjoy if you decide to play.

Yeah it's a good game, I'd recommend it in a sale. However, being that it's made by a small studio (I think it's just one dev) I wasn't against paying full release price. Try the demo atleast if you're unsure.

This was a short game (albeit playing on the lowest difficulty), but it was a good one. The tongue in cheek humor and simple combat are enjoyable, and this is a great game to kill a few hours at a time. Just remember to save before and after beating each level of the dungeon.

1.0 had some bugs, but the game is on 1.009 and the developer has been hard at work dealing with the worst of them. Overall, I highly recommend this game and look forward to the dev's future work.

The RPG is a cute little sendup to Skyrim and other fps-rpgs of its kind. It maintains a generally fun atmosphere and the base gameplay is solid, but the game is buggy and very short. Also: I couldn't beat the final boss because all his attacks one-shot me, and I just got tired of plinking away at his massive healthbar.

I think most people will get bored of this game within an hour. If you're like me and just wanted a goofy reason to bop stuff in first-person, you'll probably get your money's worth right up until the final level of the dungeon.

Not recommended for most people.

Starts out good then becomes what it makes fun of. Second half of the game is very tedious. Also it says "Carefully plan your targets, moves and ammo. Get ready to sweat!" but you can beat the game by just heavy attacking while strafing or moving back and forth. Its braindead, but mobs are very tanky in the second half.

[b]The RPG[/b] is a minimalist [strike]loot goblin simulator[/strike] dungeon-crawling adventure with light RPG elements, streamlined everything, and goofy side quests.  This is probably the dumbest game I've ever played, but I had a great time with it. 

[h3]Story[/h3]
Trapped in a strange fantasy kingdom after the Glitchy Dragon destroyed its only bridge, you must [strike]steal everything[/strike] test your mettle in the local Dungeon to convince the "Cool King" that you are, in fact, also cool.  The story is ridiculous and predictable, but entertaining.

[h3]Gameplay[/h3]
Fundamentally, a first-person quest-driven RPG: basic combat, skill points at level up, a small open world, and a multi-level dungeon with bosses and occasional platforming.  Can switch between default "Adventurer" difficulty to "Story" (deal more/take less damage) or "Challenge" at any time.

[b]Controls.[/b]  Playable with a controller, but the camera is slow even at max sensitivity.  I found keyboard/mouse to be a smoother experience. 

[b]Playtime.[/b]  In Adventurer Mode, it took me ~8 hours to complete the game and all side quests while also exploring every area of the map.  It took ~1.5 hours to speedrun Story Mode for a missed achievement. 

[h3]What Worked For Me[/h3]
✅ Option to turn off animated dialogue.
✅ Simplified combat was silly and fun (but may feel monotonous).
✅ No inventory management; useless items are converted to "loot."
✅ Explore the open world by foot, catapult, or portal.
✅ So many Easter Eggs.
✅ And [i]so much looting.[/i]
✅ The "???" level.
✅ I need an entire biography about My Fan, please and thank you.

[h3]What Didn’t Work For Me[/h3]
❎ First-person camera can be rough, especially in [spoiler]Dragon's lair[/spoiler].
❎ Occasionally froze on loading screen when entering the Dungeon.
❎ Occasional visual glitch caused loot counter to shake.
❎ Monsters sometimes froze after player death.
❎ Object rendering is janky; enemies may materialize in your path.
❎ Limited NPC dialogue.

[h3]Final Thoughts + Recommendation[/h3]
[b]The RPG[/b] opens with a shameless [i]Skyrim[/i] parody followed by the most pointless character creator (and censorship) on Steam, and keeps that vibe going throughout the campaign.  It's a lot of well-worn tropes lovingly wrapped in dumb jokes, environmental humor, basic combat, and all the jankiness you might expect from a solo dev's first game.

It's also charmingly goofy and accessible.  [b]The RPG[/b] does a great job of streamlining every crunchy aspect of traditional RPGs: junk items are converted to units of "loot" to trade for gold, while key items are displayed at the bottom of your screen; gear is upgraded progressively; most quests are resolved with minimal fuss; and combat is reduced to a few buttons, which can feel repetitive but refreshing.  There's no real penalty for getting smashed by golems, no time pressure, and plenty to discover across the map. 

I recommend checking this out if you want good dumb fun and a single-player adventure that lets you steal everything, catapult yourself across the world, and dungeon dive without thinking too hard about your character build or learning new controls.

It overstays its welcome in 2 hours. Theres not really that much to this game. Its like someone filled a checkbox in what is supposed to be in an rpg Without actually making it fun. Every system is super shallow.
Such a fun game with a lot of content. References to other games and is a basic parody of Skyrim, but it's also completely different. Worth the purchase and 100% worth playing.

**Still playing the game and haven't beaten it fully**

Very fun and simple game. The Loot mechanics are nice, i like how simple is is to upgrade your gear, many of RPG style quests that scratch my monkey brain. Absolutely love the art style. 
Very much worth the buy to support the dev. Haven't ran into any game breaking bugs or bugs in general. 

Would ask for some QOL changes like, 1. Holding "E" to pick up loot instead of pressing the button for every individual loot piece. 2. A stat screen, like how much loot, enemies kill, quest completed, shots fired, ect 3. maybe a 3rd person view or more interactive views to see my character. 4. A chat log. This one is very low on the priority list, but sometimes when i went to do a quest that involves me traveling to a different area i forget what i was told and go back to interact with the quest giver again.

Some changes id like to see in the future, but completely understand the game is new and the developer sound like they are very quick to fix bugs, and roll our updates. Also i completely understand if some of these changes go outside of how the developer wants the game direction to go. Still 100% support the game!
More upgrades  (shouts, the mushroom gun, move speed, quest rewards, xp percentile boost)
different weapons, like heavy higher damage, but slower attack speed, vs. higher attack speed and lower damage. 
A dash, adds a different way to parry rather than block. i find myself not using the block as much because its easier to move back and away from the enemies and avoid the attack all together while charging up a heavy attack

Overall great game, i love the art, style, and how unique yet simple it is. Very fun and very good for my simple monkey brain. I definitely recommend buying it to support the dev! 8.3/10!

Very silly game, plays on all the tropes from Skyrim, dark souls, etc. can be a bit buggy or cause a crash or two but not game ruining. Overall, a super silly and surprisingly fun game that I'd recommend to everyone.

It starts out funny, but shortly becomes typical RPG with few jokes, and then there is the end. It's (end) rather awful, with overpowered mobs.
Also, performance is terrible, fps drops to 5 when there are more than 7 enemies.

Overall 7/10, but worth the price.

this is a buy on sale, gameplay is very boring with extremely monotone patterns and enemies so spongy you need a new name for that effect. the writing can be funny at times but not enough to make the gameplay pill go down smooth, generaly if you like RPGs you might find some fun here but not enough for full price.

Got this game as a demo after randonly running into it on the steam store, played about a half hour before beating the first boss and getting a little extra time, but by the time i got there, i genuinely wanted to play more and I knew just an hour wasnt going to cut it. Wound up paying for the full game not even 24 hours later and needless to say im not only impressed, im completely hooked. Total rpg style adventure game with twists of OGs from the past. Has the exact elements i look for in a game. If youre a fan of skyrim and dorky adventures. This games for you!

This game is sooo fun! It doesn't take itself seriously which adds to the fun. A few bugs.. (I'm looking at you, Necromancer) but nothing that has halted any game progression. its cute and well priced

I saw this game in Steam and based on the intro video thought it would be a fun little RPG parody. Early parts of the game held up that premise- the inside jokes were fun, I liked the glitchy dragon and the cool king. But after going back and forth a dozen times doing the main questline, I realized I wasn't going to get anything else out of the experience so there wasn't really any reason to keep going back into the dungeon and interacting with the characters. I was interested in some side quests but kept getting soft locked out of them, where I'd need to talk to a character for the quest and after getting the same dialogue 20 times the quest did not progress (looking at you, Necromancer- I'm sorry that I killed your minions but c'mon).

I appreciated the humor and I think the game would be better if there was more to do + more variance in the results of the quests. The game feels a little unfinished, for me, after the first hour or so. I don't really recommend it, sadly don't feel like I got $13 worth of enjoyment out of the game but hopefully it'll get some updates and I can come back to it in a couple years.

100%'d it because its such a simple fun time.  If you're looking for a simplistic satirical take on RPGS, this is the game for you. Its nothing too deep or life changing, its just a good time, glitches allowed. The only thing I could do was move my camera, swing, and block. I had to do a hard shut down, then I came back to see my save was before getting to the dragon even though I save right before going into the dungeon. I got dropped down from lvl 34 to lvl 27. Also, I was locked out of 2 quests (freeing the adventurers from the cave and removing the bandage from the adventurers mouth). With that being said, I will be uninstalling. I do enjoy the game though. That loss of progress is just unfortunate. I'll be back to finish it after a few patches.
A must for any RPG lover.

Done - 100%.'

I had a blast with it. I was a bit worried about the length versus the price, but I'm glad I went for it. 10/10 would side with the glitchy dragon again. 

I will say that the game does require you have an appreciation of the humor at offer so try the demo first and see if its the style you enjoy. 

I only came across one bug during my playthrough (couldn't talk to the Necromancer for the quest to take the bandaid off the guys mouth) but it wasn't a progression blocker as I did everything else I came across fine. There is also auto save in the version I played so the developer got that in pretty quickly it seems.
Pretty fun, and the devs seem interested in player feedback. They've already added stuff like autosave after it was requested enough!

The NPCs walk around in the town, people react to your actions, you can steal and the guards will attack you, if this 12 Euro game can do that why couldn't Avowed do it?

Awoved was such a shitty game, i bought into the hype and believed the marketing. Obsidian is dead and everyone working on avowed should be sent to a coalmine and forced to dig with their hands, since they don't deserve the right to use any machinery or tool after the colossal feckup they have made. unfortunately those people still walk around freely and they are not forbidden from touching a computer again, so if anyone from Obsidian happens to read this, get in contact with the devs for this game, get on your knees and beg them to reveal how is it possible to make guards that can attack hostile creatures.

by the way "The RPG" is fun so far

After 11 hours of playing, I have beat the main story line along with many of the side activities, but it is definitely worth the price under $15. A couple of glitches along the way, but looking forward to move content. Highly recommend!
good

TL;DR: Overall, I think this is a short, but fun, game made by a passionate developer, and I think you should give it a try.

Skyr... I mean, The RPG is a lot of fun. I beat the game and got all achievements in 8.5 hours, but a few hours were just spent wandering around fighting monsters and discovering the island. I would say most players can finish it in 6-8 hours, and it is worth the price. I also like that you are expected to figure some things out on your own. I loved the enemy designs and the design of the dungeon, especially; my only wish is that there was more to do and explore.

My main criticism is a lack of replayability value, unless you are doing challenge runs (granted, it is an RPG-lite made by a solo developer). I also wish there were more secrets and rewards for doing things beyond the achievements. Like maybe collecting [spoiler]mushrooms[/spoiler] increases [spoiler]the damage of the mushroom gun[/spoiler], defeating a certain enemy type many times increases damage dealt to that enemy type, or there are rewards that can carry over to future new games (like [spoiler]rewards for finding all secrets[/spoiler], [spoiler]spending a certain amount of gold or unlocking all weapons or armor makes them cheaper or more powerful in future runs[/spoiler]). Maybe beating the game unlocks some speedrun or challenge modes like you can only use the [spoiler]wooden stick[/spoiler] (may not be possible) or you [spoiler]can't heal from bread[/spoiler].

I did encounter a few minor bugs, like getting temporarily trapped [spoiler]in the dungeon elevator on the lobby floor when the doors failed to open, but going to another floor and then back to the lobby fixed it[/spoiler]. I think the fact that I only encountered a few bugs is a testament to the amount of work that has gone into polishing this game.

Super cute and fun, gave me something to blow a Saturday on, I wish I could see how many of what types of bread I have, also maybe throwing another word into the title as this game title is hard to Google anything about lol!

Can't wait for the Ascended Anniversary Remastered Legendary Edition release!
if you like ragdoll, parady, and first person rpg then this is the game for you .
Great game, looking for more from this developer :)

game softlocks me out of every 2nd quest now..

cant talk to the necromancer, cant free the prisoners... cant progress the game 

gg
Good game.
Awesome little game.
been waiting for this

It's a fun game to kill a Saturday with, but I kept running into bugs and glitches, including in the final fight.

Fair warning, stock up on everything you could want before you enter the last area. You can't get back out due to a bug with a door being stuck from the inside until you kill the enemies on the outside, which you can't do unless I missed something that kills things through walls at some point.

Dev, I tried to report bugs when I could, but I kept crashing. Sorry, good luck.

I had fun with it, it really scratches the Supraland itch. I ran in to quite a few bugs, some will entirely lock you out of quests. The developer has been very responsive and is patching them, so this is a big thumbs up from me.

I need more of this game. Great simple fun. All the rpg puns from over the years, all the guards have arrows in the knees. I hope they add more stuff to the game. The default key bindings didn't feel comfortable IMO so had to change them up. I'm at the last boss right now but can't beat him due to fps drops from adds.
Really short and really fun. Excellent funny mini-RPG
nice
meh
Kind of silly but a LOT of fun. Plus under the silliness is a really well run game, see way more bugs with AAA releases
Fun, fun, fun.

I really love this game, I have finished the game and gotten all achievements but it does have bugs but they don't effect the general game Edit: Bugs have been fixed ;)
One of my favourite games in a while :)
Fantastic game 100% recommended
Really fun for what it is, highly recommend.

It's fine. I don't think anyone enjoyed the laser grid timing shit at the end of the game nor the dragon fight. For around $1/hr there are worse things you could do. I wouldn't really recommend it unless you're one of those people who are ok with a sort of mediocre 10hr indie game.
Really good game! Got me out of my gaming slump. Super silly with lots of fun references. Gameplay and story are both super engaging.
Played the demo and enjoyed it so much I immediately bought the game! Astounding work for a sole developer; fun and works great on the Steam Deck!

While this game is fun to an extent, the grind is quite tedious, and it is a lot of running back and forth between areas, not even bothering to attack enemies. 

I was really hoping for much more West of Loathing inspiration than Skyrim, and instead got a bit of a very lackluster amount of both unfortunately. If this was a 3D open world WoL, I would've offered myself in marriage to this dev.

Game is fun, but don't put your expectations too up there

Its a cute game and I hope they add more to it in the future!  One suggestion though, the quests need more detail so they are easier to figure out.  Also a compass would be awesome to have on the top of the screen.
amazing game really funny and fun i hope this one day gets a part 2 taking place on the main island thats how fun this was
good game. must try
This game is a genuinely fun RPG with a pretty great plot twist.   Highly recommend!

An absolute 10/10 experience, I highly recommend this game to anyone looking for something both incredibly fun and deeply engaging. The gameplay is immersive, the mechanics are smooth, and it keeps you hooked from start to finish.
I love this game! Goofy and fun in the best way possible! Genuinely very fun! This game also runs very well on Steam Deck.

it's very goofy and plays on the usual RPG troupes. I've played about 6 hours and experienced almost everything i could find. if you want a short game that doesn't take it self too seriously yet has surprisingly fun mechanics, give it a go. for the price, it's definitely worth it.
I love being a loot goblin. Thank you devs for supporting me.

Played for hours. It's a very simple and straight forward RPG game. A bit dry without much to it to make it very interesting or unique--but it kept my interest long enough to get to the endgame. The issue I had was the mobs kept glitching out and not dying when their HP bars ran out--I got softlocked in a respawn loop until I completely ran out of resources with no option to do anything but reload a last save, erasing hours of gameplay. I won't be playing this game ever again, I cannot recommend it to anyone (play any other RPG with a decent rating). 

My message to the developer, going forward, please respect gamers' time and energy.
reviews.developer_response: I feel very sorry for your lost progress and disappointed with the bad review :( the Auto-Save feature was added recently. The other things are getting patched on a daily basis.

reviews.review: This game is so addicting. Ive been searching for a relaxing fun game that has comedy and adventure in it. I scored the jackpot! I thought this game was on the market for quite some time, but after realising it got released yesterday im in shock. The optimization is good, no lag at all. Story is fun and intriguing and the combat feels simple, but fun as well. I like the “loot” system and no inventory is really interesting concept, but it works perfectly. Cant wait to finish the game and gather all the achievements!

A simple RPG but because of that it honestly does it better then some triple AAA RPG's.  You can make choices and they matter.  It's simple but real choices.  The combat is fun and the charm has unique charm.  Easily worth the 15 dollars but getting on sell is always smart. (Their is spiders but one you can buy bombs and explode them and they be gone so you will be fine).  I would recommend this game if you just want to kick up your feet and relax and not spend 80+ hours on a game.

The only thing is that is does have some noticable bugs like monsters that were killed their bodies just kind of follow after you die.  Doesn't always happen but it is annoying. The other thing is that I do wish their was more content in the northern side of the map but regardless those bugs will probably be fixed and I still recommand the game highly.

Very great game, reminds me a lot of one of my favorite simple rpg games 'supraland'. However there is 1 huge issue with buying day 1, I have played on my save for the better part of the past 4 to 5 hours, and have already come in contact with 2 gamebreaking bugs. 

Nonetheless, a very fun game! I would just give it some time before purchasing to allow the devs to work out some more of the kinks.
If you enjoyed Supraland, you'll enjoy this. Cute light hearted little RPG, well worth playing.

West of Loathing meets Skyrim. Yep, it's pretty much exactly that. 

Surprisingly the world is pretty rad. I enjoy exploring it and I love that my fan/bodyguard is constantly getting into fights and getting killed. 

Looking forward to digging into this over the weekend. Perfect, light hearted adventure. Feels like a distant or second cousin to Sir Whoopass as well.
Great little game, Id definitely like to see more of this, it was really fun.
Not gonna lie this is kinda great. And if the author releases mod support and shit, my god....the potential this has. I really loved this <3

The game apparently has zero auto-save. 

I played for 3 hours and enjoyed the game quite a bit. I quit, came back to continue a few hours later and there was no record of my save file.
reviews.developer_response: The auto-save feature is a work-in-progress, coming soon!

reviews.review: This game is actually really fun (and silly)! Stumbled upon it randomly and figured why not. No regrets.

It's moderately quaint, but the charm runs out quickly. Combat is repetitive, and slow. Progression happens too slowly to be engaging. The writing has next to no bite in it whatsoever. If you're looking for a simple game to while away the hours with, you can certainly do it with The RPG, but there are better games you can be doing that with, and for far cheaper.
Oblivion Remaster kinda expensive, so i guess this will do for now....heh heh Skyrim At Home edition....
Legit fun game, surprisingly so.
Good silly fun. Bash, blast and steal everything that's not nailed down! :) also don't forget about the mushrooms.
Great game, got addicted at Demo, give it a shoot buddy.
Everything I'd hoped for!  If you love Supraland, or any of its sequels, get this one now!
Hey you, you're finally awake. You were trying to cross the border, same as us? And that thief over there...

If you enjoy a game without auto-save and crashes so you have to backtrack the last hour, this is for you.

Edit: they added auto-save but still crashes and not-saved.

Edit2: enjoy doing a dungeon run where you can't manually save, just to finish the floor, getting in to the elevator and the game crashes (as it usually does in elevators).
reviews.developer_response: Really sorry to hear that... Auto-save is a work-in-progress!

reviews.review:
I've only played for a short time but I can already tell this game is special. It's just fun, simple as.

**Unfortunately I have had to change my review as I constantly get a bug that ends up crashing the game and losing whatever progress I have had since the last save. Can't seem to progress now unfortunately.
The king is a cool dude, can confirm.
fun game.

If you buy this game all your wildest fantasies will come true. 

 Maybe. Probably.
Fantastic. Hilarious. Goofy as hell. A looters wet dream.
69/10 would play and laugh again

Very enjoyable fun little tongue-in-cheek looter, spent a couple solid hours in the full game after release and a couple in the demo before that, pretty cool that i could carry on my save from it too, full of cheeky references here and there, character development is simple but the progression still feels rewarding and simplifying the inventory means more time for adventuring, and there's plenty to do all round the Island. On top of all that the combat feels really good, you have some mobility and style options, sometimes you don't even need to fight, but when you hit something it'll know about it.

A+ across the board from me personally

Surprisingly addictive!
Went in with pretty low expectations but ended up playing for over an hour without realizing it. The humor is sharp, the meta references are clever, and the minimalist style works surprisingly well. It’s like a love letter to classic RPGs but with a self-aware twist.

If you enjoy breaking the fourth wall and don’t mind a game poking fun at its own mechanics (and yours), this is definitely worth checking out. Looking forward to seeing where it goes from here!
i was the first to review this game. the game is cool like the king.
I can't recommend this game any harder, it had me laughing so hard!
you can deactivate nippel censoring! 10/10 must buy!
A Skyrim mod we did not deserve
